title: Deludente, non così efficace come promesso
body: Ho acquistato Paranix Shampoo Extra Forte Tratta & Previene Pidocchi e Lendini sperando in un rimedio rapido ed efficace, ma purtroppo sono rimasto piuttosto deluso. Il prodotto promette di agire in soli cinque minuti, ma nella mia esperienza non ha eliminato completamente i pidocchi dopo il primo trattamento. Ho dovuto ripetere l’applicazione più volte nei giorni successivi, con risultati ancora parziali. Anche se il profumo è tollerabile e la confezione da 200 ml è comoda da usare, la consistenza è molto oleosa e difficile da risciacquare: lascia i capelli pesanti e unti nonostante diversi lavaggi.Un altro punto negativo è il prezzo, che a mio parere è un po’ alto considerando l’efficacia limitata. Inoltre, il pettinino incluso non è molto robusto e si piega facilmente. Alla fine, il prodotto funziona solo in parte e richiede molto più tempo di quanto dichiarato. Non lo ricomprerei, anche se può andare bene come soluzione di emergenza.

title: Non uccide ma li soffoca, non efficace sulle lendini
body: Aiuta ma c’è di meglio Purtroppo non è efficace al 100% . Abbiamo avuto un infestazione a scuola, mia figlia ha preso i pidocchi, la trattavo con questo prodotto, ripetendo le applicazioni come da indicazione , ma mi accorgevo che non eliminava completamente le lendini. Il pettinino riesce a eliminare i pidocchi ma non riesce ad agganciare le lendini perché non è così stretto. Il risultato è che le dovete sfilare una ad una con le unghie, il che è facile che qualcuna vi scappi. Il risultato è che non elimina completamente tutto. Non uccide le lendini e pidocchi ma è un olio che tende a soffocare i pidocchi per effetto meccanico. Questo vuol dire che alcuni riescono a sfuggire se il prodotto non è stato messo in abbondanza è tenuto su almeno una mezz’ora. Si risciacqua senza usare shampoo o altri prodotti per non limitarne l’efficacia, ma il capello resta unto. Qualcosa fa ma mi sono resa conto che in farmacia a prezzi uguali si comprano prodotti che contengono sostanze che uccidono pidocchi e lendini, che se anche vi sfuggono dal pettine per lo meno sono morti. Alla luce della mia esperienza vi consiglio di prendere un prodotto di farmacia, costano uguali e sono più efficaci di questi che si trovano su Amazon .che non contengono piretrina , un antiparassitario che uccide uova e pidocchi.

title: Shampoo Anti-Pidocchi – Un Vero Salva-Vita!
body: Quando i pidocchi fanno la loro comparsa, la ricerca di una soluzione efficace e rapida diventa prioritaria. Dopo aver provato questo shampoo anti-pidocchi, posso dire con certezza che è un vero alleato in questa battaglia. L'ho usato per trattare l'infestazione di mia figlia e i risultati sono stati davvero notevoli.La prima cosa che ho apprezzato è la facilità d'uso. Le istruzioni sono chiare e semplici da seguire, il che è fondamentale in un momento di stress come questo. Non ha un odore sgradevole, cosa che spesso si riscontra in prodotti simili, e la sua consistenza permette una distribuzione uniforme sui capelli, assicurando che ogni ciocca venga trattata.Ma la cosa più importante è l'efficacia. Dopo la prima applicazione, la differenza era già evidente. I pidocchi erano visibilmente inattivi e il pettine a denti fitti, fornito nella confezione o utilizzato separatamente, ha permesso di rimuovere un'enorme quantità di pidocchi e lendini. Ripetendo il trattamento come indicato, siamo riusciti a eliminare completamente l'infestazione in tempi brevi, riducendo al minimo il disagio per mia figlia e per tutta la famiglia.Inoltre, ho notato che lo shampoo non ha irritato il cuoio capelluto sensibile di mia figlia, né ha lasciato i capelli secchi o danneggiati. Questo è un grande vantaggio, considerando che alcuni prodotti aggressivi possono peggiorare la situazione. Se vi trovate ad affrontare un problema di pidocchi, questo shampoo è senza dubbio una soluzione affidabile e delicata.Punti di Forza:Efficacia Rapida: Agisce velocemente per eliminare pidocchi e lendini.Facilità d'Uso: Istruzioni chiare e applicazione semplice.Formula Delicata: Non irrita il cuoio capelluto e non rovina i capelli.Odore Tollerabile: A differenza di altri prodotti, non ha un profumo sgradevole.Soluzione Completa: Permette di affrontare l'infestazione in modo efficace, riducendo lo stress per la famiglia.

title: Addio pidocchi in 5 minuti!
body: Il Paranix Shampoo Extra Forte è un vero salvavita per mia figlia Chiara, 10 anni e sempre pidocchiosa! All'interno della ocnfezione c'è un pettine adatto, ma io ho acquistato anche il pettine a denti stretti in acciaio: https://www.amazon.it/dp/B09F3CVND9?ref_=ppx_hzsearch_conn_dt_b_fed_asin_title_1e il risultato è fantastico: tratta e previene pidocchi e lendini in soli 5 minuti, senza stress né irritazioni.La combinazione shampoo + pettine elimina i fastidiosi ospiti e riduce drasticamente il rischio di reinfestazioni. Ormai sono pronta ogni ottobre per il ritorno a scuola senza ansia!Alla Gilda tip: usate sempre il pettine subito dopo lo shampoo. E' la mossa vincente per eliminare tutte le lendini e dormire tranquille.

title: Buono ma vi spiego come usarlo
body: Lo sciampo è utile per eliminare i pidocchi. Le istruzioni dentro il pacco non sono chiare (fanno riferimento a un lavaggio ogni sette giorni e poi parlano di copertura fino a e giorni). Però non aspettatevi che uccida subito pidocchi e le uova (ledoni). Io ho ripetuto lo sciampo 3 volte, una ogni due giorni e ho risolto. Fondamentale spazzolare manualmente per eliminare fisicamente pidocchi morti e uova. Ovviamente nel mentre ho lavato a 60 gradi tutto quello che è stato a contatto con mio figlio e messo in buste per 10 giorni i vestiti che non si potevano lavare a 60 gradi. I pidocchi in generale entro due giorni lontani dal corpo muoiono ma le uova possono resistere fino a 10 giorni prima di schiudersi. Spero vi sia stata utile questa recensione.

title: Aiuto
body: In commercio ci sono prodotti decisamente più gestibili. Ne avevo provato uno in confezione viola con pettinino. Mio marito ha sbagliato e ha ricomprato questo. Già il fatto che sia olio e che le istruzioni suggeriscano un lavaggio con detersivo dei piatti per rimuoverlo bene dai capelli la dice lunga sul prodotto. Va tenuto da 20 Min a 2h (intervallo di tempo quanto meno ampio e anche lì 2h sono mai gestibili coi bambini??) .. si professa ecologico, ma con la quantità di detergente che  ho usato e dovrò ancora usare per rimuovere questo impiastro, mi chiedo come si possa definire un prodotto ecologico..

title: Flop

Benutzung streng nach Anleitung. Da verlässt man sich auf die positiven Bewertungen und das Resultat ist mehr als enttäuschend!! Man wird auf eine Support Seite weitergeleitet, die nur wenig hilfreich ist, da wir von der Anleitung her nichts falsch gemacht haben!!!

title: Used Once and Shocked by the Hair Fall
body: 1 out of 5 stars - Used Once and Shocked by the Hair FallI am honestly very disappointed and quite worried after using this product. We applied it only once on our 2-year-old daughter, and within hours we noticed a lot of hair fall. She has never had any hair fall issues before this, not even a single hairfall, but after using this treatment just once, we found a worrying amount of hair falling out.We followed the instructions exactly as written, so this was really unexpected and stressful for us as parents. I genuinely bought this because of the good reviews, but our experience was the opposite. Maybe it works for some people, but based on what happened to our daughter, we definitely won’t be using it again.I hope this helps other parents decide, especially if you have very young children. For us, it caused more panic than relief.

title: no es efectivo
body: no me ha gustado porque el peine no ha quitado ni una sola liendre ademas de tener el producto el doble de teimpo que se recomienda.es como poner un aceite en el cabello.

title: Didnt work for us
body: I really wanted this to work for us as i really like the concept and it being more natural but it didnt work for us. It was messy to apply and dribbles down your head. My kids hated the feel. They were already fed up enough to be doing it and this made things worse. You have to leave it on up to 2hrs. We did the full 2hrs. Again didnt help the grumpyness of my kids all the waiting but its really greasy so didnt want them getting it everywhere. We watched a film. Brushing through it to get nits out after treatment was awful, all the solution goes everywhere, we did see a few dead nits so thought it had done ok.  But then we couldn't wash it out. Any gentleness with the solution was lost as we had to revert to washing up liquid as suggested on the instructions after 4 washes and it still leaving a greasy residue. I felt at the time it had done an ok job. I then checked the next day, still loads on nits. We didnt get this previously using headrin on a different occasion. It just worked and only took a 15min treatment. So i dry combed with conditioner and to be honest it was as though i hadnt treated the hair. I wouldnt recommend.

title: Read this before you buy.
body: Now to be fair I read all reviews sounded wonderful then I saw as seen on Dragons Den okay I was in. The product arrived quickly and so I prepared to use it. I read the instructions removed the silver foil blocking the dispenser and away I went. I turned the nozzle to the open position and nothing came out I had already shaken the liquid so didn't know why nothing was happening. Turns out when I squeezed the bottle the cover on the top was closing up . I cleaned it replaced it and tried again and again nothing so I removed the lid at which point the bottle poured out all over the me and the floor plus dogbed thick slimy liquid. Applied by hand from the bottle and had previously combed hair to silky soft no lugs. Left liquid about 45 mins then put provided nit comb through hair not much happened so I brushed it and had some success with the hair in my brush. Left lotion half hour again and couldn't get nit comb through my hair it was lugging so bad. For me this was a definite NO. Redeeming feature was no smell. Thoroughly disappointed and would not recommend for me and expensive option

title: Too much hair fall
body: I don’t usually leave reviews but needed to this time. Tried this product because it’s non toxic and for various hair types. My daughter who is 5 years old has very very thick curly hair. The product is very oily and gloopy and transfers on everything. For an active 5 year old it’s very hard to stop her putting her head down or against anything. After leaving the treatment on for a couple hours I started going through her hair with the nit comb provided. I was traumatised by the amount of hair fall she had! I had pre-combed her hair before applying the treatment and combed her hair again before using the nit comb. The hair fall after the treatment was crazy. Unfortunately I won’t be using this product again. Such a shame really as I had high hopes for it considering the reviews

title: Thick, sticky, greasy
body: I would not buy again. Extremely thick and greasy, ended up using the metal comb from another brand. Washing out is difficult. Will go back to buying the five minute one.

title: Waste of money
body: Disappointed that this product does not work at all. I've been through 2 bottles of the stuff over 3 months, following the instructions precisely and it has made no impact on the lice. The comb is flimsy and doesn't get through the hair. The seal was faulty on both bottles, meaning I struggled to open and get the nozzle in place. Waste of money.

title: Waste of money
body: Used twice and had to buy different product! Didn’t work! Won’t be buying this ever again waste of money

title: Unwirksam
body: Gut aufzutragen, schwer auszuwaschen, viele Shampoogänge nötig. Leider wirkungslos, auch nach mehreren Behandlungen neue Nissen zu finden.

title: Beware!

body: My twin boys were allergic to this product resulting in 3 days of taking antihistamines.  I purchased this product as I thought it was hypoallergenic however I have been greatly disappointed as I have had the worry of my both my sons health and well being after using this product

title: Nicht gut

title: Worst product I have ever used. Eurgh.
body: This product is literally like using thick syrup or tar all on your hair. Noticed lice in daughters hair so treated her and her 2 siblings just incase.They screamed from applying the product, waiting 2 hours and then right through to washing it out. Whilst it doesn't have a smell, it is by far easy to apply as the product doesn't smear nor lather. It literally sticks to where u apply it with no movement. It gets everywhere leaving grease type stains wherever the kids sat during waiting time which i am hoping will come out in a wash!Only pro was considering really just how very thick and greasy this product is, fairy liquid as it stated did cut straight through it when washing their hair!. I won't be purchasing to use for the post 7 day treatment, I will go for the typical well known household name products just for ease of application and comfort/messiness reasons!. 1/10 by best from me.

title: Do not recommend!
body: This product is 100% silicone! I caught nits at a wedding last weekend, and like in school days have had to go through the gruelling process of removal. I saw this product, and thought 'great! A one stop shop!'. It arrived, and I applied it to my head. It comes out like a thick glue, and soon enough my whole head was like I had poured a bucket of PVA over it. I waited for half an hour and then dove into the combing process. Oh my god. Within the first inch of my head, my scalp was raw with hair getting pulled out by the thick concoction! I tried to be patient, but soon realised I should not have my head covered in this rubbish, and grabbed the fairy liquid and washed it all out. I've since used the old school approach of coconut oil and a plastic bag, and a hairdryer to dry hair to burn them out. That's worked wonders and been half the (literal!) headache! Never again will I buy something 'dragons den'! More fool me!

title: Keine Wirkung

body: Nicht auf dem erforderlichen Niveau, und wir haben keine greifbaren Ergebnisse gesehen.

title: Difficult to self administer
body: I found this product very difficult to self-administer.While oil based, I did not feel it easily penetrating down to rootsI was unsure whether I had applied too much or too littleNext morning, felt renewed itchiness around ear area so of the view I had under applied or product not effective

title: Awful
body: Awful! Does not work at all, complete waste of money.

title: Posh olive oil
body: This is a very well marketed and overpriced product but you may as well use olive oil for its effectiveness.This doesn't smell or sting but it also doesn't really work.Leave in for 2 hours while using a nit comb? Who has time for that? I left it in overnight and used the comb and was still scratching the next day (albeit a bit less).In addition,  this stuff is impossible to wash out.  Dishwashing liquid- 4 washes later and my hair is still gunky. I'm so glad that I'm not trying to get this out of my three year old's hair.The chemical products kill everything in 15 minutes and then you just comb out the ones that didn't wash out.If you want a gentle 'eco' version, save your money and just use olive oil overnight in conjunction with a comb.  It is about the same level of efficacy.Would not recommend. Would not buy again.

title: Comb issues for thick hair
body: Not sure about the serum as only used once, but on 1st impressions its thick, not necessarily bad.But the smell after washing wasnt nice. I had to re-wash my daughters hair.The comb however was the mai reason for my review. Some teeth snapped on the 1st use, so not very strong at all

title: Does not kill eggs
body: Does not kill the eggs at all

title: Waste of effort and money.  Stick to patience, a nit comb and conditioner.
body: Having watched the instructional video we applied this treatment to a family of 5`s hair.  It was done precisely, and took longer than my tried and trusted version of using conditioner and a Nitty Gritty comb. I bought this solution believing that the applications would save combing time in the long run.  10 days and two applications later we're still all scratching.  Guess what, the conditioner and comb are gonna come back out and used instead.  I'm really disappointed.  I chose this to get away from the chemically smelling treatments, but it's been entirely false economy. Bought two bottles, but sealed one is being sent back.  I was utterly suckered into making this purchase as a fool for their clever marketing... There's an obvious investment made to get it to appear at the top of search listings, and the fact that it featured on Dragon's Den is another clever consumer manipulation.  There's no odour and now I wonder if it's no different to tipping veg oil all over your head 😩

title: Ineffective
body: Followed the instructions carefully…left for 2 hours, combed through and washed. Still live present. Don’t waste your money

title: DIdn't work
body: After trying everything on the market, I was desperately hoping this would work. It didn't. I found it easy to use, and not as drippy and oily as Hedrin. Leaving it for two hours wasn't a problem and It wasn't hard to get out either. One bottle lasted for three uses. I followed the video to the letter., and even used a nit comb before rinsing it out. Unfortunately, after three applications, and still using conditioner and combing inetween, it hasn't worked. In fact, it seems to make the itching worse. I think it removed the nit case, and left the nymphs behind.  Nothing works on super lice, and I am beginning to believe that nothing else but all-out chemical warfare will kill these things. I am waiting for Sklice to be made available in the UK.

title: Nits

title: What a Horrible Product!

Most importantly tho IT DID NOT WORK!!!! so please don’t waste your money! What were u thinking of Dragons Den!!!!

title: Very expensive not worth it

title: Doesn't work.
body: I have followed the instructions to the letter.. Unfortunately, this product has been completely unafectual..I do however have a tip for washing it out.. I used lemon shower gel.

title: Does not work
body: It does not work as effectively as other reviews state. In fact it barely made any difference. It’s unbelievably sticky however washing up liquid is not necessary to remove product as other review state. Just add shampoo after you have finished the treatment and slowly add water this should  remove a lot of the product. This also would not be suitable for three people with shoulder length hair. I would say use a different brand not Hedrin again that’s not effective.The nit  comb that is recommended again do not waste your money. I am trying three different treatments three days apart and combing daily. I was given the same advice as another review to use this method. It can be expensive just shop around on the internet for deals.  This is the only way to stop more eggs hatching and kill off any lice.My advice don’t believe all the reviews!!

title: Don't use it
body: Instructions were very hard to read. Went on like glue, very greasy. Instructions said to use washing up liquid to wash, took 3 washes to get most of it out. Hair didn't dry, just stayed damp and sticky, needed to wash again and still greasy.Horrible,  hair took a few days of washing each day to get rid of itWasted a lot of time and money. Don't buy it,  there are lots of other brands which are good quality and easier to use

title: Difficult to use, sticky, messy and not enough of it, sorry!
body: In high anticipation after reading the review, I thought to try this Dragon's Den choice after my daughter came home with potential nits from school. Well it was sadly quite a disappointment. Not only is it extremely sticky, oily and difficult to avoid getting stains in carpets, chairs or other, it needs to stay in for an enormous 2 hours! It is quite expensive if you have a daughter with medium/long hair. The 'serum' is very hard to distribute and my daughter's hair got to tangled using it to comb it out properly. She could not manage to sit still for 2 hours with a very oily head, so we washed it out before the recommended. So off to the Boots and get something quicker and easier to use.

title: Awful
body: This is the worst nit treatment I have used. Liquid is like glue and the comb is not great. It didn’t eradicate the problem either and left my daughter’s hair in a terrible condition. Won’t use again

title: Nicht optimal!

title: This didn’t work for me

It’s a nightmare to wash out.  The comb is terrible, the teeth break and it then scratches your head. I had to go to the chemists and buy a bottle of their head lice mousse.

title: Crap!
body: I never write reviews but was so disappointed in the product and the fact that I bought two bottles £30) and couldn’t return them made me quite angry!Bought this product with high hopes. These were shattered when I used said product. Not only a horrible sticky oily consistency but just infective. This is not a product that you want to fail!I am so angry!

title: Don't waste your money
body: Save your money and buy the cheaper stuff. Bought 2 bottles if this stuff. Read the instructions and decided to leave on for double the time 4 hours.. and when combing through the hair they was still alive. Not just 1 or 2 either. Not happy at all.

title: Läuse werden aus meiner Sicht nur betäubt
body: Habe es jetzt zum Zweiten Mal angewendet. Jedes Mal fängt es am 2. Tag wieder an zu jucken. Werden die Viecher nur ohnmächtig oder warum ist das so. Update: Ich  bin mir sicher. Aus meiner Sicht werden die Läuse bis zu 48 Stunden betäubt. Habe dieses Zeug 2 mal an meiner ganzen Familie ausprobiert. (insgesamt die 3. Behandlung) Beim zweiten mal noch dicker eingeschmiert und viel Zeit gelassen. Alle Klamotten und Bezüge direkt gewaschen. Trotzdem nach 1-2 Tagen wieder Läuse. Wenn man es schafft sofort alle Läuse raus zu kämmen,  wird man wohl zufrieden sein. Ansonsten hat man Pech gehabt und die Läuse bleiben. Sie sterben definitiv nicht von diesen Mittel. Zudem trocknet es sehr stark die Haut aus. Wir haben jetzt eine andere Lösung gefunden. Bio ist das Zeug auch nicht, wie hier manche es beschreiben. Bitte einmal nachschlagen was Biozid bedeutet.

title: Very gloopy
body: I had high hopes for this but was disappointed. When I opened the bottle trying to get the seal off was tricky it then exploded and went in my eye, not a good start. The product is far too gloopy understand why to catch the bits i assume but whilst the 2 hours it is on it drips everywhere.. when i tried to comb it through with the nit comb the product just splashed all over the place and i could not get the comb through my hair. very disappointed as I and my girls have long hair and  I was hoping for a better product than I usually use, Very disappointed I am afraid.

title: Doesn’t work
body: I bought this, quite hopeful, but after using once and head itching the next day (so worse than just using conditioner), I used the rest of the bottle for a deep long treatment hoping that would def be it…. But again, it didn’t stop the nit… was itchy within a day again. And I don’t think I even had many of them, but they keep returning. Definitely wouldn’t recommend. They shouldn’t be able to make the 100% claim when that is clearly not the case. I would’ve thought that is an advertising standards breach tbh. I have moved to conditioner or oil and overnight and keeping my finger, toes and hairs crossed!!

title: Greasy and doesn’t kill the lice
body: This product is the worst lice treatment I have used on my children’s hair!  It is so greasy and after washing it out their hair still looked so greasy.  They were due a hair cut the next day and the hairdresser had to do three shampoo’s to remove the product.  We still found alive lice after treatment.  Will definitely not buy this again.

title: Avoid - unless you want to lose two hours of your life
body: This solution needs to sit in your hair for two hours. It is thick, odourless oil, that drips down your neck, into your eyes, and into your ears for that whole time. The bottle advises to you use "detergent" to wash it out - which says it all! It took three washes with Fairy Liquid to strip this solution out of my 14 year old son's hair. He was in agony by the end of it - and his scalp is extremely sore. Avoid at all costs unless you want a miserable evening. I have no idea if it works but I'll not be using it again.

title: Dried out and ruined my hair :-(
body: I am not one to write negative reviews but I think this is important. If your hair is dyed or a little dry DO NOTUSE THIS! It's ruined my hair it's broken and dry and badly damaged. Plus it's given both me and my daughter dandruff.We used as directed, it's not due to misuse of the product.

title: Don't understand the hype
body: Followed all the instructions. Waited at the time intervals. Got some nits and eggs out but the nits were alive and they came back. Not worth the price, no different to cheaper versions. All claim to work, non really properly. Only solution really is to cut the hair short.

title: Awful - greasy
body: Horrible and greasy. It’s like honey/treacle to put on and then it doesn’t all come out once washed so will now have to rewash the grease out. It’s very messy and goes everywhere when you are coming through.Comb was awful, luckily had a metal one to use.Would not use this product again.

title: Sadly would not recommend
body: Wouldn’t recommend sadly. SO GREASY! Took about 5 washes over several weeks to get hair back to normal.

title: Horrible quality
body: This is what happened to the nit comb after the first use. As you can see i just took it out of the packaging and used it first time. Horrible plastic that broke with the first use.

title: VILE TREATMENT
body: Bad quality, unable to comb through hair as the lotion id a thick, greasy, tar like formula which stains and sits like OIL on everything! A nightmare to clean off bathroom tiles after use.Wish i had never bought it but fell for the as seen on ‘dragons den’ comment like a mug

title: Diffiicult to remove
body: Impossible to actually wash out of hair also strips out hair dye colour

title: Wish I had never purchased this
body: I am so very disappointed in this product. Catch phrase says “made by a mum whose had enough of head lice”…….. this product needs to be kept on for 2 hours!!!!!  Not only that but the writing on the product is sooooooo small and long to read (there is a lot of waffle) - I had to download a magnifier as an app to be able to read the instructions!!!! So very disappointed. Wether this works or not - I’m sending it back. Will be impossible to get my 4 year old to sit around for 4 hours with this treatment on her head!

title: Horrible
body: I don’t normally write reviews but this stuff is absolutely horrible. It’s a thik, oozy gel that is hard to get to the roots. It take a few washes to get out but the worst part is that it get everywhere in your house. We’ve washed our floors and hallway three times over three weeks and it still squeaks and is slippery. Probably the worst thing on the market right now.

title: Does not work
body: After months of different treatment I thought this was the answer to my prayers as nothing has worked , however this has done nothing to get ride of my head lice , I followed all instructions to the letter and left it in my hair for an extra hr , and combed after , although at first you think they are dead , this only stunned them for a few hrs and they your back to starching , do not waist your money another product that do not do what it's ment for ...it not only children who get head lice I'm 52

title: Horrible texture
body: I’ve bought few different brands and this one I didn’t like at all. The gluey texture was difficult to apply, the nits had to be brushed out for ages and still not all came out.  Had to re-wash hair several times. Maybe it works but definitely there are other brands which are much easier to deal with.

title: Its so strong that it cuts and peels your fingers to bleed
body: No brush no box. Just the product. This product its so strong that it made painful cuts on my fingers . I even bled and i used gloves jist didnt when passing the brush.. they should warn you about these things... it even damaged my scalp... i coudnt even close it back afterwards

title: Does not work
body: I bought 2 bottles of NitNOT to treat myself and my son.  We followed the instructions to the letter, thoroughly coating each and every strand in the NitNOT gloop.  I left mine on for 3 hours and my sons for 2 1/2 hours each time which even though it doesn't smell, is a very unpleasant feeling.  We had more than enough product as my son has very short hair, and mine is mid length and very fine. Frustratingly I still have headlice.  I would not recommend this product as it does not work.

title: Do not buy as it does not work
body: Applied lotion to hair as directed and left in for the full 2 hours.  Repeated treatment and even on the second application the lice were still there. The cost is double any other treatment and not effective at all.

title: Inefficace
body: Complètement inefficace ! Acheté mi mars, posé 2 fois sur ma fille et moi même pendant plus de 2h. Partout sur la tête, film alimentaire par dessus. Les poux sont toujours bien vivants !

title: Unfortunately allergic
body: Unfortunately after using this product my eye swelled and were incredibly itchy and still are a week later. The product had a weird texture but on the plus size my hair isn’t greasy.

title: Leaves hair very greasy looking
body: Tried this, no obvious headlice but child in my daughter's class has them so I did us all to be safe.  Absolutely impossible to wash out.  I tried washing up liquid as advised on the bottle but hasn't helped much.  Very disappointed.  I will not be using this brand again.

title: mr
body: expensive and not good… like putting glue in ur hair

title: Consigliato
body: Ottimo prodotto,efficace e a differenza degli altri prodotti se seguite scrupolosamete le indicazioni debellerete il problema,comodo il pettinino in allegato per effettuare l'operazione.

title: La soluzione ideale contro i pidocchi
body: Assolutamente la soluzione ai nostri problemi con i pidocchi! Purtroppo entrambe le mie bimbe hanno preso i pidocchi e li hanno passati anche a me...ho acquistato diversi prodotti ben più "famosi" di questo ma nessuno ha risolto il problema. E poi ho provato questo olio shampoo ed è stata la svolta. Spariti con una sola applicazione. Unica pecca lascia i capelli unti dopo l'utilizzo, ma è sufficiente un lavaggio e i capelli tornano perfetti.

title: Prodotto eccellente
body: Abbiamo usato prodotti comprati in farmacia che non hanno aiutato.Dopo il prima utilizzo ha aiutato in maniera impeccabile. Ottimo prodotto

title: Eccezionale

Ho provato tantissime sampoo ma senza alcun risultato, oppure di di pochissima durata. Dopo ogni uso dei diversi shampoo e soluzioni, lavato e cambiavo sempre lenzuola, asciugamani, peluche e tutto possibile. Ho trovato questo olio su Amazon e vedendo le recensioni ho deciso di provare. Sono rimasta con la bocca aperta dopo aver usato e pettinato i capelli a mia figlia. Quando guardavo trovavo qualche pidocchio e qualche lendine.Ma con questa soluzione ho pettinato e tolto tantissime, ma tantisime lendine… Sono passate 2 giorni dalla procedura con questa soluzione. Controllo ogni giorno, la testa è pulita, finalmente posso respirare. Dopo 7 giorni ripeto la procedura per asigurarmi che non ci siano piu.  Ho applicato l’olio per 2 ore, poi ho lavato con gel piatti 2 volte i capeli perché erano molto unti, in fine ho usato lo shampoo. Poi ho pettinato coi il pettine che viene fornito nella confezione. Grazie per questo prodotto.

title: Not worth it - ruined clothes
body: Am surprised by the number of positive reviews for this lotion. Have children with near waist length hair and despite the repeated requests from the husband to give everyone a grade 1 - I was optimistic and decided to give this lotion a try.The consistency is like castor oil or honey and took nearly a whole bottle per child (and it has to repeated a week later to catch any eggs that have hatched). Left it on between 1 and 3 hrs due to the sheer amount of time it took to comb through the hair (not the products fault) - however, as you're combing through the lotion - it sprays and drips everywhere. I put shower caps on everyone while they waited for it to work and it dripped onto their clothing despite towels over their shoulders. Fortunately, I had put a plastic sheet down but as I had sliders on - I inadvertently carried in onto the tiled floor and three of us nearly slipped on it (it was in different places...in case you wondering why we didn't clean up after the first near fall).I was initially pleased with the plastic comb (it's white, so easy to see the nits) but if there is any resistance or the hair is thick - the comb teeth bend or splay rendering it useless. My hands were covered in the solution so it continually slipped out - despite repeatedly washing my hands and using kitchen roll in between. I ended up using the Nitty Gritty comb we already had (which isn't that great either as you can't see if you're picking up nits properly).Finally, used washing up liquid once, followed by shampoo, which did the job.It removed most of the nits but all of the tops the children & I were wearing have been ruined by the splatter and drips.For the 7 day follow up, I will be switching to another brand - there are far easier choices without the mess,  expense of the lotion (esp. as it's more expensive than most) - and ruined clothes to boot.Now maybe that grade 1 isn't such a bad idea after all..

title: un trattamento non è bastato
body: Leggendo tanti commenti entusiasti e dopo aver speso tanti soldi per cercare di eliminare questi odiosi parassiti, ho comprato NItnot. All'inizio mi è sembrato un prodotto fantastico: vedendo tanti pidocchi stecchiti, mi sono detta "finalmente ci siamo! questa è la volta buona che ne usciamo!". Dopo averlo tenuto in testa per 3 ore e averle lavato i capelli con Nelsen piatti, le ho passato il pettinino con i denti stretti in ferro e ho trascorso così tutto il giorno. Poi ho cambiato tutte le lenzuola, copridivano, ecc e ho pensato di aver chiuso finalmente (dopo un anno!) con questi esserini. Dopo una settimana ho ripetuto il trattamento (qualche pidocchio neonato era di nuovo presente sulla testa di mia figlia) e pensavo di aver concluso questa odissea.  Ma dopo 10 giorni, li ho trovati di nuovo! A questo punto non mi rimane che tagliarle i capelli perchè ho veramente provato di tutto e mai (mai!) mi era successa una cosa del genere (purtroppo è la 5° volta che li prende!)

title: Not a winner for us
body: Update after 2nd treatment:I followed up a week later with a second treatment. Along with more eggs I found one very large adult louse which was either newly caught or survived the original application. But seems to have worked eventually. Increased rating as it did eventually work, even if the first application wasn’t a success.-With two kids in infant school lice are constantly making their way back into our house so wish I had the positive experience so many others had, but I found this possibly the worst I’ve tried to date.Pros:- No nasty smell (although found the same of Hedrin and Full Marks).- Found it significantly easier to remove eggs when combing through with a nit comb before washing out compared to the other brands.- Washes out as easily as any other. Did so the only way I’ve ever done it with any brand (fairy liquid straight onto hair without wetting, then rinse and repeat 2 more times before a final wash with shampoo). It takes 5 minutes and left the hair squeaky clean - although not as soft and conditioned-looking as other brands.Cons:- The only one that matters. Doesn’t kill the lice! I presume the claim that it kills 100% is based on the drowning lice in the stuff in a lab rather than real world conditions. I applied as directed, but left for 5 hours as I was sceptical. After 5 hours I saw two crawling around in her hair still very much alive. I then re-applied for 2 more hours and did a final go over with a nit comb.- Awful to apply, for sure the worst of the three brands I’ve tried. So very oily and gloopy.I’ll finish the bottles with a follow up treatment but then it’ll be back to Full Marks for us.

title: There's a method to killing lice and it doesn't really depend on the product you use
body: With two kids in primary school and one in nursery, I have been battling lice for years now and multiple times per term. I am resigned to the fact that I will be treating lice at least once a month until they're in secondary. So this is a very costly task - both in money and time. So my review is based on those two factors because after trying literally every product on the market, not a single one is 100% effective first time. And if you're expecting to do a one and done treatment, you will be repeating this cycle more often than necessary because you absolutely have to break the cycle and repeat your treatment. So in my opinion, you're better off ignoring the effectiveness claims of a product and focus more on the time and money aspects and make sure your method is correct.PROSThere are lots of pros to using this product outside of the ones shown in the listing. It is the only way to get a comb through my daughters very thick hair and I think this would be a good fit for anyone with thick, curly, or dry hair. I would never, ever, put fairy liquid on my children's hair. I don't want to deal with the damage and dryness but I can understand it's a cheap option for dissolving the oil. I have found that if you apply any shampoo directly to the hair BEFORE water, it creates a creamy lather. Add little bits of water to make it lather more, rinse and repeat 2 more times. This has been effective at getting it out.It is competitively priced for what's out there but given the cons (below) the price feels a bit high as the time required to treat is so much longer than the other options out there.CONSThis is a very time consuming option compared to other products. I like that it's eco friendly and just one ingredient but waiting 2 hours to comb and trying to keep kids away from pillows and couches (because it transfers oil everywhere) is a stressful nightmare. Some of them will keep a towel on their heads but try doing that with my 2 year old, not a chance. Washing it out takes forever too. There are shampoo based treatments out there and other oil based options that take 20 minutes or less to be 'effective'. None of them are 100% like I said so I tend to purchase the quicker ones and take an awful amount of care combing. I've even had luck using pure coconut oil with a few drops of peppermint and tea tree oil, but the same time consuming issue applies to this option (and the oily mess) but it's the cheapest option I've found.The MOST important thing you can do in this process is to repeat it in 7 days (and many times I've done a third treatment 7 days after that and found the lice still kicking). I love all of the advice provided in the listing, all of it is spot on in my experience, so don't skip any of these steps and you should be lice free in no time!I would recommend this product to people with certain hair types and older children who can be responsible and stay away from textiles. If you are short on time or have younger children, you're better off getting a 15 minute shampoo option, combing really well, and repeating in a week. It is the same result. Hope this helps!

title: Time consuming but works well and has better ingredients
body: Last week my daughter came home with the dreaded note from her teacher; her class has a nit infestation. I’d already gone through her hair with no sign of head lice but when I saw this, I loved the idea and ordered straight away. You can’t be too thorough when it comes to these things and the stress it can bring the household is not worth thinking about.The bottle contains 200ml which is more than most of the leading brands. It also has more of the key nit killing ingredient without the unhealthy addition of alcohol and solvents. The issue with those products is they can dry out the scalp and aren’t always effective on the nits.This is a clear liquid with no strong scent. It calls itself a serum, and it really is. The consistency is very slick but quite thick. The product is easy to spread over the whole of the scalp and hair. It goes a long way, so you I could easily treat my daughters long hair multiple times with one bottle.The treatment makes it easy to comb through my daughters hair. The comb slides straight through. It doesn’t foam up either, so you can see if there are any nits on the comb. You do have to leave it on for two hours (I put a shower cap on my daughter to keep it contained).Because of the serums consistency, i put clarifying shampoo straight onto her hair after treatment and before water just to help it wash out. I shampooed 3 times in total and rinsed really well. The result is her hairs In good condition and there are no nits so I’m very pleased.

title: Of all products around this one stands out
body: I am hugely impressed by the web page for this product with the easy to follow instructions and helpful questions and answers. No unrealistic claims are made and clear explanations are made about the life cycle and appearance of these depressingly common and indiscriminate pests.As products go this potion has the highest credentials and the best of ingredients that won’t cause harm or eco damage. Well done. It was good have this ready for the next inevitable infestation at the beginning of this summer’s holidays. No scent at all so not offensive to use, the thick gel sticks to all parts of the hair and scalp to do its work. After the two hours and two shampoo Ingatestone first with washing up liquid as advised, all the nits were dead and combed out. Next morning check there were none at all to be found so off we went to the barber confident that we wouldn’t be turned away. As a side note I would say that my grandson was really grateful to get this sorted as he admitted to being most embarrassed by his nit infestation let alone being upset about not being able to sleep for the itching. Bring back the nit nurse please NHS and recommend everyone to use this great product!

title: It works
body: This is a very effective nit treatment solution with the added benefit of being eco friendly. It doesnt have an overpowering chemically smell like many do. It is easy to apply and comb through using a nit comb. It also has a long shelflife so is great for keeping in the cupboard for future use. A couple things that arent so great are: the consistency is incredibly thick so even though it does work effectively, it does take some time and addtional products to wash out. Also the wait time after application is 2 hours which is a long time, especially if your plan is to use it prior to normal bath time before bed etc. Otherwise it performs well so would recommend.

title: Efficacia solo parziale, mi aspettavo molto di più
body: Ho provato Lendinout Act Trattamento Antipidocchi con la speranza di risolvere il problema in modo rapido e soprattutto delicato, visto che si tratta di un dispositivo medico senza agenti chimici. Purtroppo la mia esperienza è stata solo discreta. Il prodotto non mi ha dato la sensazione di eliminare completamente il problema già dal primo utilizzo, e anche dopo aver seguito con attenzione le istruzioni ho dovuto ripetere il trattamento più volte. Lo shampoo incluso è abbastanza normale, mentre il pettine svolge il suo lavoro ma non in modo particolarmente pratico o efficace come mi sarei aspettato. Apprezzo l’idea di una soluzione meno aggressiva, ma in questo caso il risultato finale non mi ha convinto del tutto. Per il prezzo e le promesse fatte, mi sarei aspettato un’azione più incisiva e un risultato più rapido. In sintesi, non è un prodotto da bocciare completamente, ma per me resta un acquisto solo parzialmente soddisfacente.

title: Pulita alla prima applicazione

I capelli lunghissimi di mia figlia si pettinavano senza problemi di nodi  ,fino all estremità.Il prodotto ha un odore molto pungente, quasi di benzene, ma tranquillamente Sopportabile. title: Inefficace da solo, odore forte, va usato con i guanti
body: Eccoci, come mamma ho purtroppo fatto questo spiacevole incontro e son dovuta ricorrere a varie tecniche e prodotti. Questo qui,essendo un olio,rende ovviamente facilissima la procedura col pettinino soprattutto sui capelli lunghi, si evita così la tortura degli inevitabili strappi. Sulla reale e assoluta efficacia però ho i miei dubbi. Abbiamo ripetuto il trattamento dopo un paio di giorni e i malefici sfuggiti al primo erano ancora vivi. Alternerò un trattamento anche con piretroidi x sicurezza, ma credo di avere capito che nessuno è realmente efficace, vanno usati metodi alternati di vario genere e tipo.Questi snervanti micro mostri sono una seccatura infinita e non esiste bacchetta magica purtroppo, bisogna sacrificare tantissimo tempo e tantissima pazienza! Occhio che ha un odore pungente e,soprattutto, a  me ha  fortemente corroso lo smalto delle unghie(sul cuoio capelluto x ora non ho visto irritazione x fortuna), vi consiglio vivamente di usare i guanti

title: Ottimo prezzo, ideale come soluzione di prevenzione
body: mio nipote ha preso i pidocchi e a scopo preventivo ho utilizzato questo prodotto un paio di volte. Prima si usa il pettinino a denti fitti per controllare se ci sono pidocchi (attenzione perche' il pettinino ha i denti in acciaio e puo' irritare/graffiare la cute). Poi si mette il prodotto oleoso sulla cute e capelli, facendo particolare attenzione a metterlo bene nelle zone dietro alla nuca e dietro alle orecchie. Si lascia agire per almeno 10 minuti e si risciacqua bene senza lasciare residui.Ho usato un balsamo per evitare che i capelli rimanessero troppo secchi. La consistenza del prodotto e' oleosa, si stende facilmente, non cola. L'odore non e' dei migliori ma e' accettabile.A scopo preventivo funziona bene, non so dire se elimina effettivamente pidocchi e lendini perche' fortunatamente anche grazie a questo prodotto non li ho "presi". Ottimo il prezzo su amazon a 8,50 euro anziché oltre 12 euro.
